doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_mpn_sub_aux (mpfr/src/div.c L723-L744). Subtracts {bp, n} >> extra plus carry-in `cy` from {ap, n}, in-place, and returns the borrow-out. The shift by `extra` (0 or 1) right-shifts b by `extra` bits before the limb-by-limb subtraction (with bp[1] contributing the top bit of bp[0]'s shifted form). The TS port returns the modified ap array in `result` and the borrow in `borrow` (since TS has no in-place mutation contract — the locked schema is immutable). Class 'arithmetic'.
c_signature: static mp_limb_t mpfr_mpn_sub_aux(mpfr_limb_ptr ap, mpfr_limb_ptr bp, mp_size_t n, mp_limb_t cy, int extra)
divergence_from_c: C: mutates ap in place, returns borrow as mp_limb_t. TS: returns {result: bigint[], borrow: bigint} — the modified ap is in `result`, the borrow in `borrow`. Both result and borrow are bigints in [0, 2^64). Inputs ap, bp are arrays of bigints; cy is bigint in {0n, 1n}; extra is JS number in {0, 1}. The TS port can either (a) mirror the C limb-by-limb loop with explicit carry tracking, or (b) reassemble both arrays into bigints, perform `a - (b >> extra) - cy` with bigint arithmetic, then re-split into limbs (computing borrow from any underflow). Both produce identical results.
refs: mpfr/src/div.c L723-L744 — the C reference body.
src/internal/mpn/sub_n.ts — sibling subtraction without the shift.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: GMP mpn limbs are LITTLE-ENDIAN by limb index'.
